attributes.description: Alerts when producer sends are blocked by broker memory pressure (`activemq.queue.blocked.sends` rate > 0). Any non-zero value means the broker is already out of memory headroom and is back-pressuring producers.
attributes.name: [ActiveMQ OTel] Blocked sends detected

investigation_guide.blob:
## ActiveMQ blocked sends detected

### What fired
The counter `activemq.queue.blocked.sends` registered a non-zero rate on at least one destination. Producers are being blocked while the broker waits for memory to free up.

### Why it matters
Blocked sends are the canary for broker memory exhaustion. When memory is saturated, producers stall instead of pushing new messages through; enqueue latency spikes and throughput drops. Left alone, the broker may stop accepting new persistent messages entirely.

### Triage
1. Identify the affected destinations and brokers from the alert context.
2. On the Broker Health dashboard, check `activemq.memory.utilization` — it is almost certainly above 0.85.
3. Compare enqueue vs dequeue rates (`activemq.message.enqueued` / `activemq.message.dequeued`) on the hot destinations to confirm consumer lag.
4. Check `activemq.consumer.count` on affected queues — zero or insufficient consumers is a common root cause.
5. Inspect JVM state (`activemq.jvm.memory.heap.used`, GC activity) — broker under JVM pressure will drag memory signals with it.

### Remediation
- Scale consumers or fix slow consumer code paths for the backed-up destinations.
- Raise broker memory limit if the workload has legitimately grown.
- Drain the DLQ or pressure-relief queues if they are consuming memory budget.

### Tuning
- The rule fires on *any* blocked send rate > 0, which matches the documented "zero is the only healthy value" stance. If your cluster tolerates brief bursts, require `blocked_rate > N` over a longer window.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-activemq.otel-*
// activemq.queue.blocked.sends is counter_long; dimension: activemq.destination.name
| WHERE activemq.queue.blocked.sends IS NOT NULL
| STATS blocked_rate = SUM(RATE(activemq.queue.blocked.sends)) BY activemq.broker.name, activemq.destination.name
// Any blocked sends indicate memory pressure — critical signal
| WHERE blocked_rate > 0
| SORT blocked_rate DESC
| LIMIT 10
